title: Gastridium lainzii
taxonomy: Gastridium lainzii is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. The genus Gastridium is a small, monotypic genus of grasses. Gastridium lainzii is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. Gastridium lainzii is closely related to other grasses within the family Poaceae.

intro: Gastridium lainzii is a species of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues, such as bloating and abdominal pain. Gastridium lainzii contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its potential health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Gastridium lainzii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
